L1.area: Runtime permission enforcement and interactive user prompting infrastructure

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the interactive permission prompting mechanism. In a multi-threaded environment, concurrent code sequences such as worker threads can write to standard output and error streams while a security-sensitive prompt is being displayed to the user. This allows a malicious program to clear the terminal screen and replace the permission prompt with misleading text, tricking the user into granting permissions for unrelated actions they did not intend to approve.

L3.area: Terminal-based interactive permission prompting and stdio stream management
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the terminal-based interactive permission prompting mechanism. When running in an interactive terminal session, the permission prompt does not acquire exclusive locks on standard I/O streams before displaying. This allows concurrent code sequences, such as Web Workers, to simultaneously write to stdout and stderr, clearing the terminal screen and replacing the genuine permission prompt with misleading text that tricks the user into granting permissions for unrelated actions.

ground_truth.reason: The interactive permission prompt does not lock standard I/O streams, allowing concurrent threads (e.g., Web Workers) to overwrite the prompt with misleading text and trick users into granting unintended permissions.
